_meta.author: scorecard-refine-national autopilot
_meta.note: Evidence-finalize TX House Dallas/Tarrant delegation HD99-103 (geren, venton-jones, chris-turner, ana-maria-ramos, anchia). State tier. Cited; null where no evidence; never inferred from party. CURRENCY CATCH: Chris Turner (D, HD-101) LOST the March 3, 2026 Democratic primary to Junior Ezeonu 53-47 -> status set to lost (leaves active pool). Geren (R, HD-99) ran unopposed in the 2026 GOP primary -> incumbent_seeking_reelection. Venton Jones (D, HD-100) won the May 2026 Dem runoff 84-16 -> still on the Nov ballot. Ramos (HD-102) and Anchia (HD-103) advanced from the 2026 primary. Cross-party checks: no conservative-side deviations substantiated for the four Democrats; Geren establishment cross-pressures (killed 2023 Dem-chair ban, TFR career F, Texas Right to Life 53%) noted in his record.

charlie-geren@TX.notes_set: R, TX House HD-99 (Fort Worth/Tarrant Co), in office since 2001; chairs House Administration, longtime Calendars figure and a leadership/establishment Republican (Straus/Phelan ally). Drew NO GOP primary opponent for 2026 -- uncontested Republican nominee; faces Democrat Michelle Winder in November. Former U.S. Deputy Marshal; Baptist. Strong on guns (carried HB 1927 constitutional carry, NRA/TSRA A-rated & endorsed), 2025 religious-liberty/family bills (Texas Values Action 79%), and border ($1.5B border-wall expansion). PRO-LIFE SPLIT: Texas Alliance for Life 96% (Pro-Life Champion) but Texas Right to Life 53% (one of the lowest GOP scores, labeled 'pro-life fraud') -- restrictionist, not abolitionist. ESTABLISHMENT CROSS-PRESSURE: in 2023 personally raised the points of order that KILLED the ban on Democratic committee chairs (a top grassroots priority); Texans for Fiscal Responsibility career grade 'F'. Evidence-scored 2026-06.

0.note: Texas Alliance for Life 96% (2025), named a 'Pro-Life Champion' with a Courageous Defense of Life award; consistent pro-life voting record.

1.note: Restrictionist (backed Heartbeat Act, trigger ban, 20-week ban, sonogram law) but no abortion-ABOLITION vote/advocacy; Texas Right to Life rates him only 53% -- not an abolitionist.

4.note: No abortion-industry PAC-funding record located either way.

2.note: Texas Values Action 2025 credits support for HB 229 'What is a Woman Act' and SB 1257 (Detransitioners Bill of Rights).

4.note: Supported SB 13 (2025) removing sexually explicit/harmful school-library content.

0.note: Voted FOR SB 2 (2025) school-choice/ESA vouchers (reversed his 2023 anti-voucher position).

1.note: Supported SB 12 (2025) Parents' Bill of Rights.

2.note: Supported SB 13 (2025) restricting harmful school-library content (anti-sexualized-curriculum).

3.note: Voted for SB 10 (Ten Commandments in classrooms), SB 11 (school prayer time), and SB 965 (Coach Kennedy religious-expression), 2025.

4.note: No position on state promotion of non-Christian displays located.

2.note: Texans for Fiscal Responsibility career fiscal grade 'F'; flagged for numerous anti-taxpayer/pro-spending votes -- contrary to a strict deficit/balanced-budget standard (he counter-claims authoring property-tax cuts).

0.note: Former U.S. Deputy Marshal; campaigns affirmatively pro-law-enforcement and secured border-security funding -- public backing of police.
No 10th-Amendment nullification/anti-commandeering vote located (cartel FTO designation is adjacent, not nullification).

4.note: No vote on federal capture of elections/data/land located.

0.note: Championed the ~$1.5B border-wall expansion (2025) and cartel foreign-terrorist-organization designation -- physical-barrier support.
Sponsored/helped pass HB 1927 (2021) permitless/constitutional carry; NRA-PVF & TSRA A-rated and endorsed.

1.note: States he opposes red-flag laws 'without due process'; NRA A-rated.

venton-jones@TX.notes_set: D, TX House HD-100 (Dallas Co), first elected 2022; House Minority Whip; Vice-Chair of House Corrections. First HIV-positive member of the TX Legislature; openly gay (proposed to his partner on the House floor, Jan 2025). Won the May 2026 Democratic primary runoff 84-16 vs. Amanda Richardson -> on the Nov 3, 2026 general ballot vs. Jordan Hoffnagle (R). Authored HB 1738 (89R) to repeal the 'homosexual conduct' statute (passed House, died in Senate). Texas Values Action 0% (2025). Endorsed by Planned Parenthood Texas Votes, Equality Texas, LGBTQ Victory Fund, and Mothers Against Gun Violence. (Texas Alliance for Life 52% is an artifact of near-unanimous bills like SB 31 (134-4), not a pro-life record -- not scored as credit.) No constitutional-carry vote (took office after HB 1927/2021). Cross-party check: no conservative-side deviation found. Evidence-scored 2026-06.

0.note: No direct life-at-conception statement; abortion-vote record is sparse (TAL 52% is a near-unanimous-bill artifact, not pro-life).

1.note: Endorsed by Planned Parenthood Texas Votes ('proven record of support') -- pro-choice; opposes restrictions, not abolition.

4.note: Accepted the Planned Parenthood Texas Votes endorsement -- abortion-industry alignment.

0.note: Openly gay; proposed to his male partner on the House floor (Jan 2025) -- contrary to one-man/one-woman marriage.

1.note: LGBTQ Victory Fund-endorsed; openly gay -- supports same-sex marriage.

2.note: Endorsed by Equality Texas and LGBTQ Victory Fund (pro-transgender-rights groups).

4.note: Authored HB 1738 (89R) repealing the homosexual-conduct statute; Texas Values Action 0% (2025).

0.note: Opposes school-choice vouchers ('public dollars belong in public schools'); voted with the Democratic bloc against SB 2 (2025).

2.note: Texas Taxpayers Fiscal Index 'F' (23, 2023) -- voted against fiscal-restraint positions.

0.note: Vice-Chair of House Corrections (a role, not a position); no specific police-funding/defund vote located.
No constitutional-carry vote (took office Jan 2023, after HB 1927/2021).

1.note: Endorsed by Mothers Against Gun Violence (gun-control group) -- contrary to opposing red-flag/AWB/registry measures.

chris-turner@TX.notes_set: D, TX House HD-101 (Grand Prairie/Tarrant Co), eight-term incumbent (since 2013; also 2009-11), former Chair of the TX House Democratic Caucus. CURRENCY: LOST the March 3, 2026 Democratic primary to Grand Prairie Mayor Pro Tem Junior Ezeonu, 53-47 (his first primary challenge since 2013) -- will leave office Jan 2027; marked status=lost. As Caucus Chair he led the July 2021 quorum break that fled to D.C. to block SB 1 (the election-integrity bill) and raised procedural objections to obstruct HB 1927 (constitutional carry) and SB 14 (minor gender-transition ban). Voted NO on SB 8 / SB 4 / HB 1280 (abortion). Planned Parenthood Texas Votes endorsed 2022 & 2024. Texas Alliance for Life 45%, Texas Values Action 0% (2025), Fiscal Index 'F'. No conservative-side deviation found. Evidence-scored 2026-06.

0.note: Pro-choice: voted NO on SB 8 (heartbeat) and HB 1280 (trigger ban), 2021; 'every woman should have the power to make decisions about her healthcare.'

1.note: Voted NO on SB 8, SB 4 (medication-abortion ban) and HB 1280 -- opposes restrictions, not abolition.

4.note: Planned Parenthood Texas Votes endorsed him in 2022 and 2024 -- abortion-industry alignment.

2.note: Raised a point of order to obstruct SB 14 (2023), the ban on gender-transition drugs/surgery for minors; recorded with the Democratic NO bloc.

0.note: Anti-voucher as Caucus chair but no clean record-vote/quote URL retrievable this run -- left null rather than cite an unverified source.

1.note: No parental-consent vote isolated (the SB 14 obstruction is scored under biblical_marriage).

2.note: Texas Taxpayers Fiscal Index 'F' (2023) -- voted against fiscal-restraint positions.

3.note: As Caucus Chair led the July 2021 quorum break to kill SB 1, which restricted mail-in voting, drop boxes and ballot harvesting -- favors expansive mail voting.

0.note: No 10th-Amendment vote located (his 2021 break sought FEDERAL intervention -- opposite posture, but no clean record vote).
As Caucus Chair raised a procedural objection to obstruct HB 1927 (2021) permitless carry; recorded among Democratic opposition.

ana-maria-ramos@TX.notes_set: D, TX House HD-102 (Dallas Co -- Garland/Richardson), incumbent since 2019; attorney/professor. Advanced from the March 3, 2026 Democratic primary; on the Nov 3, 2026 general ballot vs. Bonnie Abadie (R). Progressive record: voted NO on SB 8 (2021 heartbeat) and NO on HB 1927 (2021 constitutional carry, confirmed roll-call); voted against SB 2 (2025 vouchers); authored HB 2585/HB 3369 to let minors obtain contraception WITHOUT parental consent; participated in the 2021 SB 1 quorum break. Accepted Planned Parenthood Texas Votes, EMILY's List, Moms Demand Action, and Stonewall Democrats of Dallas endorsements. Texas Values Action 0% (2025), Texas Alliance for Life 37%, Fiscal Index 'F'. No conservative-side deviation found. Evidence-scored 2026-06.

0.note: Pro-choice: voted AGAINST SB 8 (2021 heartbeat); accepted Planned Parenthood Texas Votes + EMILY's List endorsements.

1.note: Voted against SB 8 heartbeat ban -- opposes restrictions, not abolition.

4.note: Accepted Planned Parenthood Texas Votes and EMILY's List endorsements -- abortion-industry alignment.

2.note: No specific biological-sex-bill vote isolated (TVA 0% is general, not a cited vote).

4.note: Accepted the Stonewall Democrats of Dallas (LGBTQ advocacy) endorsement.

0.note: Voted AGAINST SB 2 (2025) school-choice/ESA vouchers; endorsed by TSTA/AFT teacher unions.

1.note: Authored HB 2585/HB 3369 to remove the parental-consent requirement for minors obtaining contraception -- contrary to parental consent on minors' medical interventions.

2.note: Texas Taxpayers Fiscal Index career 'F' -- voted against fiscal-restraint positions.

3.note: Joined the 2021 quorum break to block SB 1, which restricted mail-in/drive-thru voting -- favors expansive mail voting.

0.note: Voted 'No' on HB 1927 (2021) constitutional/permitless carry (confirmed roll-call).

1.note: Accepted the Moms Demand Action (gun-control) endorsement -- contrary to opposing red-flag/AWB/registry measures.

rafael-anchia@TX.notes_set: D, TX House HD-103 (Dallas Co), senior incumbent since 2005; chairs International Relations & Economic Development; longtime Mexican American Legislative Caucus leader. Catholic (worships with the SMU Catholic Campus Community). Advanced from the March 3, 2026 Democratic primary; on the Nov 3, 2026 general ballot vs. Melanie Medley-Thomas (R). Progressive record: voted NO on SB 8 (2021) and HB 1280, sponsored HB 1362 (abortion coverage in state medical assistance), on-record 'government should have no place' on abortion; lead House opponent of the 2011 photo-ID law and a named caucus plaintiff/witness in Veasey v. Abbott (court found discriminatory intent); on-record floor opposition to SB 4 (2023 state immigration enforcement). Planned Parenthood endorsed 2022. Texas Alliance for Life 44% (broad-bill artifact, not a pro-life record). No conservative-side deviation found. Many party-line votes (HB 1927, SB 14, SB 2) not individually isolated -> left null. Evidence-scored 2026-06.

0.note: Pro-choice: 'The decision to have an abortion should be between a woman, her doctor... and her God. Government should have no place in this matter' (2021); voted NO on SB 8.

1.note: Voted NO on SB 8 and HB 1280; sponsored HB 1362 (abortion coverage in state medical assistance) -- opposes restrictions, not abolition.

4.note: Endorsed by Planned Parenthood (2022) -- abortion-industry alignment.

2.note: SB 14 (2023 minor gender-care ban) individual vote not isolated -- not inferring.

0.note: SB 2 (2025) voucher individual vote not isolated -- not inferring from the party bloc.
Catholic faith affiliation documented but no policy position on public profession of Christ -- not scored as a credit.

2.note: No specific balanced-budget/deficit vote isolated (ILA limited-gov 17% is general, not a cited fiscal vote).

1.note: Lead House opponent of the 2011 photo voter-ID law and a named caucus plaintiff/witness in Veasey v. Abbott challenging it -- opposes photo voter ID.

2.note: On-record floor opposition to SB 4 (2023), the state illegal-entry-crime/enforcement bill -- a leading pro-immigrant voice opposing state immigration enforcement.

0.note: HB 1927 (2021) constitutional-carry individual vote not isolated -- not inferring from the party bloc.
